# UC-22.29.12 · Remediation and root-cause tracking

## Description

Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for remediation and root-cause tracking aligned with APAC breach laws.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for AU Privacy Act, PIPL compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes privacy and sourcetypes breach:event. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel. Indexes required: `index=privacy`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=breach:event`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="breach:event" jurisdiction IN ("SG","TH","JP","KR","CN") earliest=-180d
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval authority=strptime(notified_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval individuals=strptime(notified_individuals_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval sla_h=case(jurisdiction="SG",72,jurisdiction="TH",72,jurisdiction="JP",120,1=1,24+12)
| eval breach=if(isnull(authority) AND (now()-detected)>sla_h*3600,1,0)
| eval register_line="12"
| table breach_id, jurisdiction, breach, register_line
```

Understanding this SPL

**Remediation and root-cause tracking (APAC breach laws)** — Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for remediation and root-cause tracking aligned with APAC breach laws.

Documented **Data sources**: Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: privacy; **sourcetype**: breach:event. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=privacy, sourcetype="breach:event", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **detected** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **authority** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **individuals** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_h** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **breach** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **register_line** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Output fields: authority, breach, breach_id, detected, individuals, jurisdiction, register_line, sla_h.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the GRC system and the audit register, and with regional reporting cutoffs in your runbook. Document any expected regulatory reporting window or approved exception before escalating.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).

## SPL

```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="breach:event" jurisdiction IN ("SG","TH","JP","KR","CN") earliest=-180d
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval authority=strptime(notified_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval individuals=strptime(notified_individuals_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval sla_h=case(jurisdiction="SG",72,jurisdiction="TH",72,jurisdiction="JP",120,1=1,24+12)
| eval breach=if(isnull(authority) AND (now()-detected)>sla_h*3600,1,0)
| eval register_line="12"
| table breach_id, jurisdiction, breach, register_line
```
